overview: This database is named maintenance and contains all the Operational data for a 3D-printing (FDM) plant's preventive and reactive maintenance It holds the machine fleet and their versions/manuals, staff, spare-parts inventory, technician availability, preventive-service history, and reported incidents with their agentic diagnoses and resolutions taken during the maintenance process.

employees.description: All staff at the plant across three roles — operators (who report incidents via the chat medium), technicians (who perform maintenance and resolve incidents), and supervisors (who oversee escalations).
employees.when_to_use: The main purpose of this table is to look up a person's role, their current employment status, their contact email for notifying them when an incident is booked, the shift in which they are working, e.g. to find a technician to assign, get the requester's email to notify, or check whether someone is active.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique staff identifier.

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: Employee's full name.

columns.name: role
columns.description: Job role — operators report faults, technicians resolve them, supervisors oversee/escalate.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Email address used for notifications.

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Contact phone number.

columns.name: shift_time
columns.description: Working shift window — supervisors work 9AM-5PM; others rotate across the three 8-hour shifts.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Whether the employee is currently active (assignable) or inactive.

columns.name: date_joined
columns.description: Date the employee joined the organization.

incidents.description: The entire case record for reactive, incident-driven faults — one row per reported incident. Captures the full lifecycle: the operator's confirmed complaint, the agent's root-cause and proposed resolution, the allocated technician, the technician's comments, and the closure date.
incidents.when_to_use: To review past or live incidents for a machine or update the reported incident post booking it — what was reported, how the agentic workflow diagnosed and resolved it, who fixed it, and whether it's closed or still open. Useful as prior-incident context when diagnosing a new fault.
incidents.notes: An open/live incident has technician_comments and incident_closure_date as NULL (technician allocated but work not yet completed); a closed incident has both filled. The scheduled work date/slot (work_date, work_slot) is the day and window the assigned technician — or, on escalation, the supervisor — is booked to do the work, distinct from when the incident was reported or closed.

columns.name: incident_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for an incident (e.g. inc_1).

columns.name: machine_id
columns.description: The machine the fault occurred on.

columns.name: reported_date
columns.description: Date the incident was reported.

columns.name: reported_time
columns.description: Time the incident was reported.

columns.name: reported_by
columns.description: Operator who reported the incident.

columns.name: user_complaint
columns.description: The agent's understanding of the user's query, confirmed by the user before the incident is posted.
columns.example: Printer halted mid-print showing a MINTEMP error.

columns.name: agent_root_cause
columns.description: Root cause identified by the agentic workflow.
columns.example: Hotend thermistor wiring broken or disconnected.

columns.name: agentic_resolution
columns.description: Solution proposed by the agentic workflow.
columns.example: Inspect and replace the hotend thermistor; secure the wiring connection.

columns.name: technician_id
columns.description: Technician (or escalated supervisor) allocated to resolve the incident.

columns.name: work_date
columns.description: Scheduled day the allocated technician/supervisor is booked to do the work (may differ from reported_date). NULL until a slot is booked.

columns.name: work_slot
columns.description: Scheduled time window for the work, e.g. 09:00-11:00 (a 2-hour technician slot, or a 1-hour supervisor slot on escalation). NULL until booked.

columns.name: technician_comments
columns.description: Tasks the technician performed; usually confirms the agentic resolution. NULL while the incident is open.
columns.example: Confirmed to agentic resolution; performed as advised and verified the fix.

columns.name: incident_closure_date
columns.description: Date the incident was closed. NULL while the incident is open.

inventory.description: Spare-parts stock for the FDM printers — the parts a technician would draw on to resolve incidents or perform maintenance (hotend components, motion parts, electronics, bed parts, and consumables such as filament).
inventory.when_to_use: To check whether a part is in stock, how many are on hand, where it is stored (bin), whether it is at/below its reorder threshold, and which machine versions it fits — e.g. before assigning a repair, confirm the needed part is available.

columns.name: part_id
columns.description: Unique part identifier.

columns.name: part_name
columns.description: Human-readable name of the spare part.

columns.name: category
columns.description: Part grouping (Hotend, Motion, Electronics, Bed, Extruder, Consumable).

columns.name: compatible_mvc
columns.description: Which machine versions the part fits (ALL or a comma-separated list of MVC codes).

columns.name: quantity_on_hand
columns.description: Current number of units in stock.

columns.name: reorder_threshold
columns.description: Stock level at/below which the part should be reordered (low-stock signal).

columns.name: unit
columns.description: Unit of measure (pcs, rolls).

columns.name: bin_location
columns.description: Storage location/bin where the part is kept.

machine_versions.description: Registry of the distinct machine versions (models) the plant owns, each identified by a Machine Version Code (MVC). The spine of the schema — links physical machines to their manuals and service intervals.
machine_versions.when_to_use: To resolve a machine's version into its model, manufacturer, firmware, recommended service interval, or the path to its instruction manual (the RAG source document).

columns.name: mvc_code
columns.description: Machine Version Code — unique identifier for a machine model/version.

columns.name: model_name
columns.description: OEM model name.

columns.name: machine_type
columns.description: Equipment category (all FDM 3D printers in this dataset).

columns.name: manufacturer
columns.description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer.

columns.name: manual_path
columns.description: Relative path to this version's user-manual PDF (the RAG source document).

columns.name: firmware
columns.description: Firmware the version runs (context for firmware error messages such as MINTEMP).

columns.name: service_interval_days
columns.description: Recommended preventive-maintenance interval, in days; drives the overdue check.

columns.name: date_onboarded
columns.description: Date this version was added to the registry.

machines.description: The physical-unit FDM machine (printer) asset registry — every individual printer on the floor (named as M01, M02, …), each tagged to its machine version (mvc_code). The model/manual/interval details come from machine_versions via mvc_code. It also has the physical location of the printer.
machines.when_to_use: To identify a specific machine the user refers to, confirm it exists, find its version (to reach the right instruction_manual/specs), its physical location, current operational status, or when it was installed — e.g. validate "M03" and resolve it to its version before diagnosing.

columns.name: machine_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for a physical machine/unit.

columns.name: mvc_code
columns.description: The machine's version code — links to its model, manual, and service interval.

columns.name: serial_number
columns.description: OEM serial number of the unit.

columns.name: location
columns.description: Physical location of the machine within the plant (floor/cell).

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current operational state of the machine.

columns.name: install_date
columns.description: Date the machine was installed/commissioned.

maintenance_history.description: The log of PREVENTIVE (regular ~21-day) services performed on machines. Reactive / incident-driven work is not recorded here — it lives in the incidents table.
maintenance_history.when_to_use: To find a machine's preventive-service history — most importantly the latest preventive service date, which (plus the 21-day interval) drives the overdue check; also to see who serviced it and what was done.
maintenance_history.notes: Preventive cadence is driven only by the previous preventive service date — reactive/incident work does not reset it. Overdue check = latest preventive service_date + the version's service_interval_days (21) vs. today.

columns.name: service_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for a service record (e.g. serv_1).

columns.name: machine_id
columns.description: The machine that was serviced.

columns.name: service_date
columns.description: Date the preventive service was performed.

columns.name: performed_by
columns.description: Technician who performed the service.

columns.name: technician_comments
columns.description: Notes on the work done (the standard preventive SOP).

technician_schedule.description: A monthly availability calendar of technicians for working on incidents — one row per active technician per working day. Each row gives that technician's daily 2-hour window (inside their shift) for taking on incident work, and whether that slot is still free.
technician_schedule.when_to_use: To find when a technician is available to be assigned an incident — check which technicians have an Available slot on a given date, and book one by flipping its status to Booked.

columns.name: date
columns.description: The working day this availability row is for.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: The technician this slot belongs to.

columns.name: shift_time
columns.description: The technician's shift that day (sourced from employees); the slot falls within it.

columns.name: availability_slot
columns.description: The 2-hour incident-work window for that day (e.g. 09:00-11:00).

columns.name: availability_status
columns.description: Whether the slot is free or already booked for an incident; the agent flips it to Booked when assigning.
